dropPdf: Drop PDF files here or click to select
encryptedPdf: This PDF is encrypted and cannot be merged
corruptedPdf: This PDF file is corrupted and cannot be read

descriptions.title: About PDF Merger
descriptions.aeoDefinition: PDF Merger is a free online tool for combining multiple PDF files into a single document. Drag-and-drop to reorder, preview thumbnails, and download the merged result. All processing runs locally in your browser.
descriptions.whatIs: Combine multiple PDF files into a single document directly in your browser. Upload your PDFs, drag to reorder, toggle individual files on or off, and download the merged result. No data is uploaded to any server — all processing uses the pdf-lib library.
descriptions.useCasesP1: Combine multiple reports, invoices, or contracts into a single file for easy sharing.
descriptions.useCasesP2: Merge scanned documents or printouts into one cohesive PDF.
descriptions.step1Text: Drag and drop your PDF files or click to select them from your computer.
descriptions.step2Text: Drag to reorder files. Toggle off any files you want to exclude from the merge.
descriptions.step3Text: Click the Merge button to combine all enabled files into a single PDF and download it.
descriptions.faq1Q: Is there a file size limit?
descriptions.faq1A: No. PDF merging runs entirely in your browser, so there is no server-side file size limit. Very large files may take longer to process.
descriptions.faq2Q: Can I reorder pages within a PDF?
descriptions.faq2A: No. This tool merges entire PDF files in the order you arrange them. Each PDF is treated as an atomic unit — all its pages stay together.
descriptions.faq3Q: Are my files uploaded to a server?
descriptions.faq3A: No. All processing happens locally in your browser using the pdf-lib library. Your files never leave your device.
